title: Tapping for Money, Sex, Power: The Taboo Trio
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the cultural rule that women must choose between financial power and being acceptable
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the cultural conditioning that links money, sex, and power into a taboo for women. The script addresses the guilt or fear that arises when claiming financial authority feels at odds with being 'a good woman', and works toward the settled ownership of both.
sourceNotes: Original tapping script written for homemade.education. Draws on EFT framework (Gary Craig, mid-1990s). The intersection of money, power, and female identity is documented in feminist economic psychology literature (see: Harriet Lerner, Ann Fels) and in Rebecca J Page's MONEY (2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when claiming money, authority, or desire triggers guilt — or when you notice yourself shrinking one to protect the other.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the cultural rule that women who claim money and power should expect to pay for it somewhere else. The script works with the guilt or self-monitoring that arises when financial authority and desire feel like they belong to different women — not the same one.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though part of me believes that women with money have to be less something else, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though claiming financial power feels like it costs something I can't name, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've absorbed a rule that says money and desire and authority don't all belong to me at once, I honour what that's been protecting, and I am open to releasing it now.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: the rule that says good women don't want this much.
Side of eye: the idea that claiming power means losing something softer.
Under eye: the guilt about wanting money and desire at the same time.
Under nose: the self-monitoring every time I step into authority.
Chin: the old story that links money and power to something unbecoming.
Collarbone: I took the rule in early. I didn't invent it.
Under arm: I am allowed to set it down now.
Top of head: the cost of holding it is higher than the cost of releasing it.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I get to want it all. That's not greed — it's wholeness.
Side of eye: money and desire and authority belong to the same woman.
Under eye: I claim my financial self without apology.
Under nose: I step into authority without muting myself.
Chin: the rule was never mine to hold.
Collarbone: I give myself permission to own all of it.
Under arm: I am whole — financially, personally, fully.
Top of head: I am the woman who has money and is herself.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

Written for homemade.education. Uses Gary Craig's EFT tapping framework, in use since the mid-1990s. The intersection of money, power, and female identity is documented in feminist economic psychology literature and explored in Rebecca J Page's MONEY (2025).
